commit_message: Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module and fix register logic Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module and fix register logic Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module to improve register handling and fix bugs related to control and digest registers. Update register offsets and logic for better clarity and functionality.

examples.commit_message: Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module and fix register logic Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module and fix register logic Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module to improve register handling and fix bugs related to control and digest registers. Update register offsets and logic for better clarity and functionality.

+/**
+ * @file EF_SHA256_driver.h
+ * @brief Firmware driver for EF_SHA256 IP core with Wishbone interface
+ * 
+ * This driver handles all bugs/quirks in the EF_SHA256_WB wrapper:
+ * - Sets mode bit for SHA-256 (vs SHA-224)
+ * - Handles reversed block/digest register order
+ * - Optionally clears CTRL after init (auto-clear in fixed wrapper)
+ * - Provides helper functions for padding and multi-block hashing
+ * 
+ * USAGE EXAMPLE:
+ * 
+ *   // Single-block hash
+ *   EF_SHA256_init(SHA256_BASE);
+ *   uint32_t digest[8];
+ *   EF_SHA256_hash_string("abc", digest);
+ *   
+ *   // Multi-block hash
+ *   EF_SHA256_hash_init();
+ *   EF_SHA256_hash_update(block1, 64);
+ *   EF_SHA256_hash_update(block2, 64);
+ *   EF_SHA256_hash_final(last_data, remaining_len, digest);
+ */
+
+#ifndef EF_SHA256_DRIVER_H
+#define EF_SHA256_DRIVER_H

+#include <stddef.h>
+#include <string.h>
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * Register Offsets
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+#define EF_SHA256_STATUS_REG    0   /* Read-only: bit 6=ready, bit 7=digest_valid */
+#define EF_SHA256_CTRL_REG      1   /* bit 0=init, bit 1=next, bit 2=mode */
+#define EF_SHA256_GCLK_REG      2   /* Clock gate enable */
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK0_REG    3   /* Block registers 0-15 (REVERSED ORDER!) */
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK1_REG    4
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK2_REG    5
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK3_REG    6
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK4_REG    7
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK5_REG    8
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK6_REG    9
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK7_REG    10
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK8_REG    11
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK9_REG    12
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK10_REG   13
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK11_REG   14
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK12_REG   15
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK13_REG   16
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK14_REG   17
+#define EF_SHA256_BLOCK15_REG   18
+#define EF_SHA256_DIGEST0_REG   19  /* Digest registers 0-7 (natural order) */
+#define EF_SHA256_DIGEST1_REG   20
+#define EF_SHA256_DIGEST2_REG   21
+#define EF_SHA256_DIGEST3_REG   22
+#define EF_SHA256_DIGEST4_REG   23
+#define EF_SHA256_DIGEST5_REG   24
+#define EF_SHA256_DIGEST6_REG   25
+#define EF_SHA256_DIGEST7_REG   26
+
+/* Control bits */
+#define EF_SHA256_CTRL_INIT         (1 << 0)  /* Start new hash */
+#define EF_SHA256_CTRL_NEXT         (1 << 1)  /* Continue multi-block */
+#define EF_SHA256_CTRL_MODE_SHA256  (1 << 2)  /* Mode: 0=SHA-224, 1=SHA-256 */
+
+/* Status bits */
+#define EF_SHA256_STATUS_READY        (1 << 6)  /* Core ready for new operation */
+#define EF_SHA256_STATUS_DIGEST_VALID (1 << 7)  /* Digest output is valid */
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * Global Base Address (set by init function)
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+static volatile uint32_t *EF_SHA256_BASE_PTR = NULL;
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * Low-level Register Access
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+static inline void EF_SHA256_write_reg(uint32_t offset, uint32_t value) {
+    EF_SHA256_BASE_PTR[offset] = value;
+}
+
+static inline uint32_t EF_SHA256_read_reg(uint32_t offset) {
+    return EF_SHA256_BASE_PTR[offset];
+}
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * Initialization
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+/**
+ * @brief Initialize SHA256 peripheral
+ * @param base_addr Base address of SHA256 peripheral (e.g., 0x30000000)
+ */
+static inline void EF_SHA256_init(volatile uint32_t *base_addr) {
+    EF_SHA256_BASE_PTR = base_addr;
+    
+    /* Enable clock gate */
+    EF_SHA256_write_reg(EF_SHA256_GCLK_REG, 1);
+    
+    /* Wait for ready */
+    while (!(EF_SHA256_read_reg(EF_SHA256_STATUS_REG) & EF_SHA256_STATUS_READY));
+}
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * Block Write Helper (handles reversed order)
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+/**
+ * @brief Write a 512-bit block to SHA256 input registers
+ * @param block Array of 16 x 32-bit words in STANDARD order (W[0]..W[15])
+ * 
+ * NOTE: This function handles the reversed register order internally.
+ *       Caller provides data in standard order: W[0] first, W[15] last.
+ */
+static inline void EF_SHA256_write_block(const uint32_t block[16]) {
+    /* Core expects REVERSED order:
+     * W[0]  → BLOCK15_REG (offset 18)
+     * W[15] → BLOCK0_REG  (offset 3)
+     * 
+     * So we write block[i] to BLOCK(15-i)_REG
+     */
+    for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
+        EF_SHA256_write_reg(EF_SHA256_BLOCK0_REG + (15 - i), block[i]);
+    }
+}
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * Digest Read Helper
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+/**
+ * @brief Read 256-bit digest from output registers
+ * @param digest Array of 8 x 32-bit words to store result (H0..H7)
+ */
+static inline void EF_SHA256_read_digest(uint32_t digest[8]) {
+    for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
+        digest[i] = EF_SHA256_read_reg(EF_SHA256_DIGEST0_REG + i);
+    }
+}
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * Core Operations
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+/**
+ * @brief Start SHA-256 hash computation (single block or first block)
+ */
+static inline void EF_SHA256_hash_init_hw(void) {
+    /* Write CTRL with init=1, mode=1 (SHA-256) */
+    EF_SHA256_write_reg(EF_SHA256_CTRL_REG, 
+                        EF_SHA256_CTRL_INIT | EF_SHA256_CTRL_MODE_SHA256);
+    
+    /* Optional: Explicitly clear CTRL (auto-clears in fixed wrapper) */
+    EF_SHA256_write_reg(EF_SHA256_CTRL_REG, 0);
+}
+
+/**
+ * @brief Continue SHA-256 hash computation (subsequent block)
+ */
+static inline void EF_SHA256_hash_next_hw(void) {
+    /* Write CTRL with next=1, mode=1 */
+    EF_SHA256_write_reg(EF_SHA256_CTRL_REG, 
+                        EF_SHA256_CTRL_NEXT | EF_SHA256_CTRL_MODE_SHA256);
+    
+    /* Optional: Explicitly clear CTRL */
+    EF_SHA256_write_reg(EF_SHA256_CTRL_REG, 0);
+}
+
+/**
+ * @brief Wait for hash computation to complete
+ */
+static inline void EF_SHA256_wait_valid(void) {
+    while (!(EF_SHA256_read_reg(EF_SHA256_STATUS_REG) & EF_SHA256_STATUS_DIGEST_VALID));
+}
+
+/**
+ * @brief Wait for core to be ready for next operation
+ */
+static inline void EF_SHA256_wait_ready(void) {
+    while (!(EF_SHA256_read_reg(EF_SHA256_STATUS_REG) & EF_SHA256_STATUS_READY));
+}
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * SHA-256 Padding Functions
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+/**
+ * @brief Pad message according to SHA-256 spec (single block, up to 55 bytes)
+ * @param message Input message (up to 55 bytes)
+ * @param msg_len Message length in bytes (0-55)
+ * @param padded Output: 16 x 32-bit words (512 bits) in BIG-ENDIAN
+ * @return 0 on success, -1 if message too long for single block
+ */
+static inline int EF_SHA256_pad_single_block(const uint8_t *message, 
+                                              size_t msg_len, 
+                                              uint32_t padded[16]) {
+    if (msg_len > 55) return -1;  /* Need multi-block for longer messages */
+    
+    /* Clear output */
+    memset(padded, 0, 64);
+    
+    /* Copy message bytes (big-endian word packing) */
+    for (size_t i = 0; i < msg_len; i++) {
+        int word_idx = i / 4;
+        int byte_idx = 3 - (i % 4);  /* Big-endian */
+        padded[word_idx] |= ((uint32_t)message[i]) << (byte_idx * 8);
+    }
+    
+    /* Add padding bit (0x80) */
+    int pad_word = msg_len / 4;
+    int pad_byte = 3 - (msg_len % 4);
+    padded[pad_word] |= 0x80 << (pad_byte * 8);
+    
+    /* Add length in bits (last 64 bits = last 2 words) */
+    uint64_t bit_len = msg_len * 8;
+    padded[14] = (uint32_t)(bit_len >> 32);
+    padded[15] = (uint32_t)(bit_len & 0xFFFFFFFF);
+    
+    return 0;
+}
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * High-Level API
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+/**
+ * @brief Compute SHA-256 hash of a short string (up to 55 bytes)
+ * @param message Input string
+ * @param digest Output: 8 x 32-bit words (256 bits)
+ * @return 0 on success, -1 on error
+ * 
+ * Example:
+ *   uint32_t hash[8];
+ *   EF_SHA256_hash_string("abc", hash);
+ *   // hash[0] = 0xBA7816BF, hash[1] = 0x8F01CFEA, ...
+ */
+static inline int EF_SHA256_hash_string(const char *message, uint32_t digest[8]) {
+    size_t len = strlen(message);
+    if (len > 55) return -1;
+    
+    /* Pad message */
+    uint32_t block[16];
+    if (EF_SHA256_pad_single_block((const uint8_t*)message, len, block) != 0)
+        return -1;
+    
+    /* Write block */
+    EF_SHA256_write_block(block);
+    
+    /* Start hash */
+    EF_SHA256_hash_init_hw();
+    
+    /* Wait for completion */
+    EF_SHA256_wait_valid();
+    
+    /* Read result */
+    EF_SHA256_read_digest(digest);
+    
+    return 0;
+}
+
+/**
+ * @brief Pad final block for multi-block hash (56-64 bytes)
+ * @param data Input data (up to 64 bytes)
+ * @param data_len Data length (0-64)
+ * @param total_bits Total message length in bits (for final padding)
+ * @param block Output: 16 x 32-bit words
+ * @return 0 on success, -1 on error
+ */
+static inline int EF_SHA256_pad_final_block(const uint8_t *data,
+                                            size_t data_len,
+                                            uint64_t total_bits,
+                                            uint32_t block[16]) {
+    if (data_len > 64) return -1;
+    
+    /* Clear block */
+    memset(block, 0, 64);
+    
+    /* Copy data */
+    for (size_t i = 0; i < data_len; i++) {
+        int word_idx = i / 4;
+        int byte_idx = 3 - (i % 4);
+        block[word_idx] |= ((uint32_t)data[i]) << (byte_idx * 8);
+    }
+    
+    /* Add padding bit if space in this block */
+    if (data_len < 56) {
+        int pad_word = data_len / 4;
+        int pad_byte = 3 - (data_len % 4);
+        block[pad_word] |= 0x80 << (pad_byte * 8);
+        
+        /* Add length */
+        block[14] = (uint32_t)(total_bits >> 32);
+        block[15] = (uint32_t)(total_bits & 0xFFFFFFFF);
+    } else if (data_len < 64) {
+        /* Add padding bit, length goes in next block */
+        int pad_word = data_len / 4;
+        int pad_byte = 3 - (data_len % 4);
+        block[pad_word] |= 0x80 << (pad_byte * 8);
+    } else {
+        /* Block is full, padding bit goes in next block */
+    }
+    
+    return 0;
+}
+
+/**
+ * @brief Compute SHA-256 hash of arbitrary-length data
+ * @param data Input data
+ * @param len Length in bytes
+ * @param digest Output: 8 x 32-bit words (256 bits)
+ * @return 0 on success, -1 on error
+ */
+static inline int EF_SHA256_hash_bytes(const uint8_t *data, 
+                                       size_t len, 
+                                       uint32_t digest[8]) {
+    if (len <= 55) {
+        /* Single block case */
+        uint32_t block[16];
+        if (EF_SHA256_pad_single_block(data, len, block) != 0)
+            return -1;
+        
+        EF_SHA256_write_block(block);
+        EF_SHA256_hash_init_hw();
+        EF_SHA256_wait_valid();
+        EF_SHA256_read_digest(digest);
+        return 0;
+    }
+    
+    /* Multi-block case */
+    uint64_t total_bits = len * 8;
+    size_t offset = 0;
+    int first_block = 1;
+    
+    /* Process complete 64-byte blocks */
+    while (len - offset >= 64) {
+        uint32_t block[16];
+        
+        /* Pack 64 bytes into 16 words (big-endian) */
+        for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
+            block[i] = ((uint32_t)data[offset + i*4 + 0] << 24) |
+                      ((uint32_t)data[offset + i*4 + 1] << 16) |
+                      ((uint32_t)data[offset + i*4 + 2] << 8)  |
+                      ((uint32_t)data[offset + i*4 + 3] << 0);
+        }
+        
+        EF_SHA256_write_block(block);
+        
+        if (first_block) {
+            EF_SHA256_hash_init_hw();
+            first_block = 0;
+        } else {
+            EF_SHA256_hash_next_hw();
+        }
+        
+        EF_SHA256_wait_valid();
+        offset += 64;
+    }
+    
+    /* Process final block with padding */
+    size_t remaining = len - offset;
+    uint32_t block[16];
+    
+    if (EF_SHA256_pad_final_block(data + offset, remaining, total_bits, block) != 0)
+        return -1;
+    
+    /* Check if we need one or two final blocks */
+    if (remaining >= 56) {
+        /* Need two blocks: one for data+0x80, one for length */
+        EF_SHA256_write_block(block);
+        
+        if (first_block) {
+            EF_SHA256_hash_init_hw();
+            first_block = 0;
+        } else {
+            EF_SHA256_hash_next_hw();
+        }
+        
+        EF_SHA256_wait_valid();
+        
+        /* Final block with just length */
+        memset(block, 0, 64);
+        if (remaining == 64) {
+            block[0] = 0x80000000;  /* Padding bit */
+        }
+        block[14] = (uint32_t)(total_bits >> 32);
+        block[15] = (uint32_t)(total_bits & 0xFFFFFFFF);
+    }
+    
+    /* Write final block */
+    EF_SHA256_write_block(block);
+    
+    if (first_block) {
+        EF_SHA256_hash_init_hw();
+    } else {
+        EF_SHA256_hash_next_hw();
+    }
+    
+    EF_SHA256_wait_valid();
+    EF_SHA256_read_digest(digest);
+    
+    return 0;
+}
+
+/* ============================================================================
+ * Utility Functions
+ * ============================================================================ */
+
+/**
+ * @brief Convert digest to hex string
+ * @param digest Input: 8 x 32-bit words
+ * @param hex_str Output: 65-byte buffer (64 hex chars + null terminator)
+ */
+static inline void EF_SHA256_digest_to_hex(const uint32_t digest[8], 
+                                           char *hex_str) {
+    const char hex_chars[] = "0123456789abcdef";
+    
+    for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
+        uint32_t word = digest[i];
+        for (int j = 0; j < 8; j++) {
+            hex_str[i*8 + j] = hex_chars[(word >> (28 - j*4)) & 0xF];
+        }
+    }
+    hex_str[64] = '\0';
+}
+
+/**
+ * @brief Compare digest with expected value
+ * @param digest Computed digest
+ * @param expected Expected digest
+ * @return 1 if match, 0 if mismatch
+ */
+static inline int EF_SHA256_digest_matches(const uint32_t digest[8], 
+                                           const uint32_t expected[8]) {
+    for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
+        if (digest[i] != expected[i])
+            return 0;
+    }
+    return 1;
+}
+
+#endif /* EF_SHA256_DRIVER_H */

examples.commit_message: Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module and fix register logic Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module and fix register logic Refactor EF_SHA256_WB module to improve register handling and fix bugs related to control and digest registers. Update register offsets and logic for better clarity and functionality.
